_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Severely hypertensive patients, BP control is suboptimal despite optimal fluid removal and use of multiple antihypertensive agents

preauth.label: Clinical notes including evaluation findings and planned line of treatment
USG/CT scan, +/- FNAC/Biopsy /IVP (for Open.) confirming the need for surgery reports

ppd_preauth.q: Was the Clinical notes including evaluation findings and planned line of treatment submitted?
Was the USG/CT scan, +/- FNAC/Biopsy /IVP (for Open.) confirming the need for surgery reports submitted?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed indoor case papers with daily vitals and treatment details available?

Was the Post procedure X ray submitted?
Was the Detailed Procedure / Operative Notes submitted?
Was the Detailed discharge summary submitted?

common_queries: Clinical notes and/or imaging (USG/CT scan, FNAC/Biopsy/IVP) are not indicative of the proposed nephrectomy procedure for benign conditions.
Clinical notes including evaluation findings and planned line of treatment not submitted for pre-authorization.
USG/CT scan, +/- FNAC/Biopsy /IVP reports confirming the need for surgery not submitted for pre-authorization.
Intra operative Photograph not submitted at claim submission.
Histopathology report not submitted at claim submission, preventing confirmation of benign pathology.

additional_information.special_empanelment: Tertiary care and availability of Laparoscopic surgery facility.
clinical_key_pointers: Nephrectomy: Surgical removal of a kidney, either to treat various kidney diseases or to remove a normal healthy kidney from a donor as part of a kidney transplant procedure.
Simple nephrectomy: is the surgical removal of a kidney.
Open Nephrectomy: A surgical procedure involves a transperitoneal incision or an extraperitoneal incision, depending on the size, location of the tumour, and the patient's habitus.

Laparoscopic Nephrectomy: is a minimally invasive surgery, both transperitoneal and retroperitoneal techniques can be used. laparoscopic nephrectomy compared with open nephrectomy, which is associated with significant morbidity and mortality.
